title: project.lifecycle.transition
description: Project-scoped wrapper around lifecycle.transition (spec 002). Server fills entityType='project' and derives actionLabel from the edge when omitted. Returns project-scoped errors layered onto the shared envelope.

Actor.description: Who initiated the transition. actor='system' is permitted by the contract schema but the server enforces that system actors may only use edges entering or leaving 'blocked' (GRILL spec 009 A4), PLUS the deterministic invariant-driven setup_incomplete → ready auto-transition (R-Ready-Trigger). Any other system-actor edge is rejected server-side with transition.refused.

ErrorCode.description: Project-scoped error codes layered on the spec 002 ErrorEnvelope. project.* codes are specific to this contract; the others are re-exported from lifecycle.transition for caller convenience. Note: state.unchanged is NOT an error code; a no-op transition returns status='noop' with no auditId and no error (GRILL A2, spec 002 noop pattern).

message.description: Human-readable explanation. MUST NOT include secrets or absolute filesystem paths.

details.description: Structured context. transition.refused MUST include allowedNextStates. prepared_source.required MUST include sourceCount. plan.required MUST include planId when one is recommended.

actionLabel.description: Optional override. When omitted the server derives the label from the (from, to) edge per spec 009 research R2.

planId.description: Approved FilesystemPlan reference. Required on edges whose side_effect is non-null (see data-model.md transition table).

allOf.description: R-PlanGated-Schema: Schema-layer enforcement that planId is present for plan-gated edges. Belt-and-suspenders alongside spec 002 plan-requirement edge table. Server still enforces the requirement authoritatively. Note: archived→ready (R-Unarchive) and archived→processing require planId only when files move (C7 criterion); the schema cannot model that condition-dependent requirement, so the if/then here only covers unconditionally-required edges (prepared, archived terminal). The server enforces the C7 conditional requirement for the unarchive edges.

Response.description: Project lifecycle transition response. status='noop' is returned when nextState equals the project's current state; no auditId and no error are present in this case (GRILL A2, spec 002 noop pattern).

then.description: No-op response: nextState equals current state. No auditId and no error. Caller should treat this as a success with no observable state change.
